steps: Start with the given equation: \(b - 72 = \dfrac{x}{y}\). The goal is to isolate \(x\).

• Choice A: \(x = \dfrac{b-72}{y}\) — this divides by \(y\) rather than multiplying; incorrect.
  • Choice B: \(x = by - 72\) — only the \(b\) term was multiplied by \(y\), not the constant 72; incorrect.
  • Choice C: \(x = \dfrac{by-72}{y}\) — simplifies to \(b - \dfrac{72}{y}\), which is not equivalent to the original equation; incorrect.
  • Choice D: \(x = by - 72y\) — this is exactly \(y(b-72)\), which follows directly from multiplying both sides by \(y\). That matches choice D — D is correct.
